title: Oncaglossum pringlei
taxonomy: Oncaglossum pringlei is classified under the family Asclepiadaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as milkweeds or dogbanes. The genus Oncaglossum contains a single species, Oncaglossum pringlei. This species is native to Mexico and is a member of the Apocynaceae clade within the order Gentianales. Within the Asclepiadaceae family, Oncaglossum pringlei is part of the tribe Asclepiadeae. The classification of Oncaglossum pringlei provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants.
uses: Oncaglossum pringlei has been found to have potential medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its full range of benefits. Traditional uses include treating various ailments such as fever and respiratory issues.
